problems.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock (One Transaction)
problems.description: You are given daily prices of a stock. Choose **one** buy day and **one** later sell day to **maximize profit**. If no profit is possible, the answer is `0`.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: `n`. Line 2: `n` non-negative integers — prices in order.
hints: Track the minimum price seen so far while scanning left to right.
Candidate profit at day i is price[i] minus that minimum.
O(n) one pass; no need to try every pair.

starterCode.cpp:
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std; int main(){int n; cin>>n; return 0;}

problems.title: Count Subarrays with Sum Exactly K
problems.description: Given an integer array (which may contain negatives) and an integer `k`, count the number of **contiguous** subarrays whose elements sum to **exactly** `k`.
hints: Prefix sum P[i]; subarray sum (i,j] = P[j]-P[i].
For each j, count how many earlier i have P[i] = P[j] - k.
Hash map frequency of prefix sums; watch prefix 0 before the array starts.

problems.title: Find All Numbers Disappeared in an Array
problems.description: You have an array of `n` integers where each value is in `[1, n]`. Some values may appear twice, others once. List all integers in `[1,n]` that **do not** appear in the array. Print them in **ascending** order, space-separated.
problems.outputFormat: Space-separated missing numbers (or empty line if none).
hints: Negate index marking or use a boolean array of size n+1.
Cycle sort / place each number x at index x-1 is O(n) in-place.
Avoid O(n) extra space with clever sign flipping on indices.

problems.title: Set Matrix Zeroes
problems.description: Given an `m×n` matrix of integers, if an element is `0`, set its **entire row and column** to `0`. Print the transformed matrix: first line `m n`, then `m` lines with `n` space-separated integers.
problems.outputFormat: Line 1: `m` `n`. Then `m` lines of transformed rows.
hints: Use first row/column as markers with a flag for whether row0/col0 should zero.
Two-pass: mark rows/cols to zero, then apply.
O(1) extra space beyond output is achievable.

problems.title: Spiral Order Matrix
problems.description: Traverse an `m×n` matrix in **clockwise spiral** order starting at the top-left. Print all elements in visit order, space-separated on **one** line.
hints: Maintain top, bottom, left, right boundaries and shrink after each edge.
Handle last row/column carefully when m≠n to avoid duplicates.
Simulate direction: right, down, left, up.

problems.title: Sort Array By Parity (Stable)
problems.description: Reorder the array so that **all even integers come before all odd integers**. Within the even group and within the odd group, **preserve the original relative order** (stable partition).
hints: Two output arrays (evens and odds) concatenated preserve stability.
In-place stable partition is harder; extra array is fine at this size.
Single pass: append to even or odd list by n%2.

problems.title: Find the Duplicate Number
problems.description: An array of length `n+1` contains integers in `[1, n]` with **exactly one** value appearing twice (others once). Find the duplicate value.
hints: Treat indices as a linked list: i → nums[i]; cycle reveals duplicate.
Binary search on value range counting how many ≤ mid.
Pigeonhole guarantees collision.

problems.title: Container With Most Water
problems.description: Given non-negative integers `height[i]` as vertical lines, pick two lines with the x-axis to form a container. Find the **maximum area** of water the container can hold.
hints: Two pointers at both ends; always discard the shorter side.
Area = width × min(height[left], height[right]).
Greedy move is safe because keeping the short side cannot beat current best with this width.

problems.title: Next Permutation
problems.description: Given a sequence of **distinct** integers, print the **next lexicographically greater** permutation on one line (space-separated). If the current order is already the largest, print the **smallest** permutation (sorted ascending).
hints: From the right, find first i where nums[i] < nums[i+1].
Swap nums[i] with smallest larger element on the right, then reverse suffix.
If no such i, reverse whole array.

problems.title: Rotate Image 90 Degrees Clockwise
problems.description: Given an `n×n` matrix, rotate it **90° clockwise**. Print `n` then `n` rows of the rotated matrix.
problems.outputFormat: Line 1: `n`. Then `n` lines of rotated matrix.
hints: Transpose then reverse each row equals 90° clockwise.
Or rotate four cells in place layer by layer.
New[i][j] = old[n-1-j][i].

problems.title: Longest Subarray of 1s After Flipping K Zeros
problems.description: Binary array `nums` (only 0 and 1). You may flip at most `k` zeros to 1. What is the **maximum length** of a contiguous subarray that can contain only 1s after optimal flips?
hints: Sliding window: expand while zeros in window ≤ k, shrink when > k.
Track count of zeros in current window.
Equivalent: longest subarray with at most k zeros.

problems.title: Minimum Size Subarray Sum (Positive Array)
problems.description: Given an array of **positive** integers and a target `s`, find the **minimal length** of a contiguous subarray with sum **≥** `s`. If none exists, print `0`.
hints: Expand right with running sum; shrink left while sum ≥ s to minimize length.
All positive → window sum is monotone when you move pointers.
Prefix sums + binary search also works.

problems.title: Reverse Words in a String
problems.description: Given a string with words separated by spaces, return the words in **reverse order**, single space between words, **no** leading or trailing spaces. Words are non-empty sequences of visible ASCII (no inner spaces).
problems.outputFormat: One line — reversed word order, trimmed.
problems.constraints: Input contains only letters, digits, and spaces; at least one word.
hints: Trim, split on whitespace runs, reverse the list, join with single space.
In-place character reverse tricks exist but split is clearer.
Watch multiple consecutive spaces in input.

starterCode.cpp:
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std; int main(){string s; getline(cin,s); return 0;}

problems.title: Add Binary Strings
problems.description: Given two binary strings `a` and `b` (no leading zeros except the single character `"0"`), return their sum as a **binary string**.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: binary string `a`. Line 2: binary string `b`.
hints: Simulate elementary school addition from right with carry.
Pad the shorter string mentally with leading zeros.
Avoid converting full integers if language limits apply — digit DP style.

problems.title: Multiply Two Numbers as Strings
problems.description: Given two **non-negative** integers represented as decimal strings (no leading zeros except `"0"`), print their product as a string **without** leading zeros (unless the product is zero).
problems.outputFormat: One line — product as decimal string.
problems.constraints: 1 ≤ |num1|,|num2| ≤ 500, inputs represent values that fit in output length limits.
hints: Grade-school multiply: each digit of one against all digits of the other.
Result length ≤ len1+len2; accumulate into an int array.
Karatsuba is overkill here.

problems.title: Compare Version Numbers
problems.description: Compare two version strings `v1` and `v2` consisting of non-negative numeric components separated by `.`. Compare numerically component-wise (ignore leading zeros in each component). Print `-1` if v1 < v2, `1` if v1 > v2, `0` if equal.
hints: Split by '.', parse ints, pad with zeros to same length virtually.
Compare left to right until difference.
Trailing .0 components are zero.

problems.title: Longest Palindrome Length (Rearrange Letters)
problems.description: Given a string of lowercase letters, you may **rearrange** characters freely. What is the **maximum length** of a palindrome you can build?
hints: Count letters; each pair contributes 2 to length.
At most one character with odd count can sit in the middle.
Sum floor(count/2)*2 plus one if any odd frequency.

starterCode.cpp:
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std; int main(){string s; cin>>s; return 0;}

problems.title: Reorganize String (No Adjacent Equal)
problems.description: Given lowercase string `s`, rearrange letters so that **no two adjacent** characters are equal. If multiple valid strings exist, print the **lexicographically smallest** one. If none exists, print exactly `IMPOSSIBLE`.
problems.outputFormat: One line — valid rearrangement or `IMPOSSIBLE` (uppercase, exact).
hints: If any character frequency > ⌈n/2⌉, impossible.
Greedy: place most frequent with spacing — max-heap by count.
Alternate placing the two highest remaining counts.

starterCode.cpp:
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std; int main(){string s; cin>>s; return 0;}

problems.title: Remove All Adjacent Duplicates In String
problems.description: Repeatedly remove **adjacent** equal letter pairs until no more can be removed (stack reduction). Print the final string. Only lowercase letters.
problems.outputFormat: One line — reduced string (may be empty).
hints: Use a stack: push if different from top, else pop.
One linear pass can simulate all rounds.
Think of it as cancelling matching neighbors repeatedly.

starterCode.cpp:
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std; int main(){string s; cin>>s; return 0;}

problems.title: Count Binary Substrings (Equal Blocks)
problems.description: Given binary string `s`, count how many **non-empty** substrings have **equal** numbers of consecutive `0`s grouped on the left and consecutive `1`s grouped on the right (or the mirrored pattern) — i.e. substrings of the form `0...01...1` or `1...10...0` with both runs positive and same length. (Classic LeetCode 696 style count.)
hints: Run-length encode: track lengths of alternating blocks.
Each adjacent pair of blocks contributes min(len0,len1) substrings.
O(n) single pass.

starterCode.cpp:
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std; int main(){string s; cin>>s; return 0;}
